items.title: Enhanced Top-Up Clarity and Model Pages in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the user experience in the models directory by clarifying top-up options with detailed pricing information. Each tier button now displays the credit amount alongside its dollar cost, and every modality has its own dedicated model page. Additionally, internationalization keys have been added for better localization support.

items.title: Boosted Catalog with 21 New Chat Models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces 21 new public chat models to the catalog, enhancing the existing offerings significantly. The addition includes models from GLM, GPT-5.x, Gemini, and Claude, while also implementing a new embedding modality for improved functionality. The logo fallback has been removed, ensuring all logos are sourced directly from the database, streamlining the user experience.

items.title: Enhance Document Pages with SSR and Build Resilience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements server-side rendering for all 469 non-private document pages, enhancing SEO and performance. Key changes include improved error handling during static site generation, ensuring that a single error won't block the entire deploy. The build process has been optimized to handle 544 routes efficiently, significantly reducing build time.

items.title: Refactor Pricing Logic for Enhanced Efficiency in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the pricing logic by creating a thin wrapper around `@acedatacloud/core/pricing`. Key functions like `getConsumption` and `formatConsumption` are now utilized directly from the core, while the currency-aware formatters are built from the store's exchange and currency. The changes streamline the pricing process and maintain exported names and types for consistency.

items.title: Streamline Operator Code with BaseOperator in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the API, service, proxy, and application operators to extend the shared BaseOperator from @acedatacloud/core, reducing code duplication. Key methods like getAll, get, create, and update are now inherited, resulting in a cleaner codebase with a net reduction of 77 lines of code. The dependency on @acedatacloud/core has been updated to version 0.7.0, ensuring compatibility with the latest features.

items.title: Refactor X402 Helpers for Cleaner Code in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The `solana.ts` utility file has been refactored to import pure, unit-tested X402 helpers from `@acedatacloud/core/x402`, reducing the codebase by 39 lines without changing any functionality. This update enhances maintainability while ensuring that all critical signing and submission logic remains intact and local. The dependency on `@acedatacloud/core` has been updated to version 0.8.0.

items.title: Refactor to Use Core X402 Helpers in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The `solana.ts` utility file has been refactored to import pure, unit-tested X402 helpers from `@acedatacloud/core/x402`, reducing code complexity by 35 lines. Key functions like `uint8ArrayToBase64` and `resolveRpcUrl` are now utilized, while critical payment functions remain untouched. This update also bumps the core dependency version to 0.8.0, ensuring compatibility with the latest features.

items.title: Streamline Operators with BaseOperator in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the API, Application, and Service operators to extend the shared BaseOperator from @acedatacloud/core, reducing code duplication. Key CRUD methods are now inherited, resulting in a cleaner codebase with a net reduction of 50 lines of code. The update also bumps the core dependency to version 0.7.0, ensuring compatibility and improved functionality.

items.title: Refactor API Code Dialog for Core Integration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the ApiCodeDialog component to eliminate local template imports and leverage the core's VARIANTS and renderApiCode functionalities. The dialog's UI and language icons remain unchanged, ensuring that generated snippets are byte-identical. All builds and lint checks have successfully passed.

items.title: Enhance Service Pages with SSR and JSON-LD in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions 69 non-private service pages to server-rendered content, eliminating the need for headless-Chrome prerendering. Key improvements include robust error handling during API calls, enhanced SEO with structured data, and a streamlined process for generating meta descriptions. These changes ensure faster load times and better crawlability for search engines.

items.title: Revamped Semantic Storyboard and Unified Style in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: The latest update introduces a semantic storyboard that aligns each narration line with its corresponding visual, ensuring content and visuals match perfectly. A unified art direction has been established across all frames, alongside the integration of the latest model versions, enhancing the overall quality and diversity of outputs. Additionally, refreshed videos and improved briefs have been added to support these changes.

items.title: Increase COMPOSE_HTTP_TIMEOUT for smoother deployments in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the `COMPOSE_HTTP_TIMEOUT` to 600 seconds to address deployment failures caused by the default 60-second timeout during large image pushes to GHCR. The change is made in the deploy workflow configuration, ensuring more reliable deployments without interruptions. This adjustment aims to enhance the overall stability of the deployment process.

items.title: Refactor Pricing Logic in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the pricing logic by consuming the latest version of `@acedatacloud/core/pricing`. Key functions like `getConsumption`, `getPrice`, and `getPriceString` are now streamlined, improving maintainability while preserving existing signatures. The build passes successfully with vue-tsc and vite.

items.title: Enhance SEO with JSON-LD Integration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces build-time JSON-LD for Organization and FAQPage directly into prerendered HTML, improving SEO visibility for crawlers. Key changes include the addition of a new script for JSON-LD generation and modifications to existing scripts to ensure structured data is included in both the static output and the shell. The implementation has been verified, setting the stage for future enhancements in data handling.

items.title: Introducing shared X402 payment primitives in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `@acedatacloud/core/x402`, a set of pure, deterministic payment helpers and constants that were previously duplicated in Nexior and PlatformFrontend. Key functions like `createTransferCheckedData` and constants such as `SOLANA_MAINNET_RPC` are now centralized, ensuring consistency and reducing code duplication. The update includes 13 unit tests to verify the accuracy of these functions, paving the way for smoother integration in both applications.

items.title: Streamline CRUD Operations with BaseOperator in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `BaseOperator<TModel, TListResponse, TDetailResponse>`, a reusable REST CRUD boilerplate for domain operators. It simplifies the implementation of common operations like `getAll`, `create`, and `update`, allowing apps to inject their own axios instance and customize methods as needed. The new structure enhances code maintainability and reduces redundancy across the Nexior and PlatformFrontend applications.

items.title: Exciting Updates with Zero Hand-Stitched Frames in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: The latest version introduces zero hand-stitched frames, utilizing gpt-image-2 to wrap real API results into beautifully designed scenes. Key enhancements include a long AceData logo for intros and outros, along with a 1:1 narration mapping to distinct visuals. Additionally, three refreshed videos have been added to the materials, showcasing the new capabilities.

items.title: Add brand-logo fallback for providers without a Service icon in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fallback logo for providers lacking a Service icon, specifically addressing the deepseek service. A curated logo from the Nexior CDN will now be used when no real icon is available, ensuring a consistent user experience. Additionally, a new test has been added to verify this functionality, with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Exciting New API Code Generator in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `@acedatacloud/core/code-snippet` module, featuring Mustache templates and a comprehensive language/library variants matrix. The new `renderApiCode()` function streamlines API code generation by deduplicating logic previously found in multiple applications. The release also includes 36 tests and publishes version 0.6.0 upon merging.

items.title: Updated Buyback History with Latest Transaction in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: The latest ACE buyback transaction has been added to the buyback history, reflecting a spend of 74.37 USDC for 160,800 ACE. This transaction occurred on June 15, 2026, and has been verified on-chain. The JSON structure has been updated to ensure clarity and compliance with the schema.

items.title: Enhance Pricing Logic with JsonLogic and Currency Formatter in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new pricing utilities in the Common Frontend, including `getConsumption` for evaluating JsonLogic cost rules and `formatConsumption` for formatting consumption values. Additionally, a currency-aware price formatter is added, allowing for flexible price string generation without store dependencies. The changes are backed by 31 tests and will publish version 0.5.0 upon merge.

items.title: Revamp Model Detail Page with New Features in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the public model detail page by implementing a dark gradient hero banner, improved loading indicators, and a more visually appealing playground. Additionally, recharge tier tabs and explanatory hints for top-ups have been added, ensuring a smoother user experience. All changes are dynamic and utilize backend data, keeping the frontend clean and efficient.

items.title: Exciting v10 Updates: No Fake Logos & Auto Theme Colors in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: Version 10 introduces significant enhancements, including the removal of fake logos, now featuring the authentic AceData mark. The auto theme color feature utilizes GPT vision for dynamic branding, while API results are now presented in visually appealing galleries. Additionally, each capability is modularized into individual scripts, improving flexibility and usability.

items.title: Enhance API Model Extraction with OpenAI Support in Backend 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces per-API model extraction for all non-private media APIs, significantly enhancing the catalog by surfacing previously missing OpenAI models like dall-e-2/3 and tts-1. The changes include deduplication by model ID and improved logo attribution, resulting in around 130 models being cataloged. Tests have been updated to ensure the accuracy of the new model listings.

items.title: Upgrade Element Plus for Better Component Sharing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update upgrades Element Plus from version 2.2 to 2.14, aligning it with PlatformFrontend and enabling shared components like Pagination. Key changes include renaming event handlers and type adjustments in Pagination and OAuthApps components. The vendor chunk size has increased significantly, raising potential performance concerns for the login page.

items.title: Refactor i18n Integration for Enhanced Localization in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the latest i18n factories from core 0.4.0 into AuthFrontend, replacing the previous inline loader and language setting methods with more efficient alternatives. The core functionality for loading locale messages and setting the language remains intact, ensuring a seamless transition. This change finalizes the i18n extraction across all three applications, improving localization support.

items.title: Refactor i18n Loaders for Enhanced Localization in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the i18n implementation by adopting the latest core 0.4.0 factories. It replaces the previous inline nested loader and `setI18nLanguage` with new methods, streamlining the localization process while preserving existing function signatures. The changes result in a cleaner codebase with a reduction of 58 lines of code, ensuring continued functionality and improved maintainability.

items.title: Refactor i18n Loaders for Enhanced Localization in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the i18n implementation by adopting the latest core factories from @acedatacloud/core. The inline flat-key loader and setI18nLanguage have been replaced with more efficient methods, while maintaining existing functionality for locale loading and language setting. Overall, this change reduces code complexity and improves maintainability.

items.title: Enhance Chrome Health Checks for Self-Healing Pods in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the health check mechanism for Chrome in prerender pods by implementing a probe that checks Chrome's DevTools endpoint. This change ensures that if Chrome dies, Kubernetes will automatically restart the pod, preventing persistent 504 errors for crawlers. Additionally, the server-side logic has been refined to maintain structured data while stripping unnecessary scripts from the rendered HTML.

items.title: Upgrade Axios and Adopt Core HTTP Client in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update upgrades Axios from version 0.21 to 1.x, aligning it with Nexior and PlatformFrontend. It also adopts the `createHttpClient` from @acedatacloud/core, ensuring consistent HTTP handling across all applications. The changes are low-risk, with no breaking dependencies, and have been verified through successful builds and tests.

items.title: Exciting Updates to the Video Factory in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a fully functional brand-short video factory, complete with a material library and three product videos. Key enhancements include a brief-driven production pipeline, integration of real provider logos, and the addition of various marketing materials. The expanded skill documentation now provides comprehensive guidance on utilizing the new features effectively.

items.title: Revamped UI for Models with New Features in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fresh design for the models section, incorporating a dark gradient banner, interactive recharge tabs, and enhanced modality icons using Font Awesome. Additionally, real logos are now displayed on each card, and hover effects have been improved for better user interaction. The changes aim to enhance the overall user experience and streamline model selection.

items.title: Enhanced multimodal catalog with private exclusions and unit fixes in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a per-model multimodal catalog, expanding non-private services into approximately 125 distinct models. Private services have been excluded to streamline the catalog, and unit corrections have been implemented for accurate pricing representation. The changes enhance scalability and prepare the frontend for a redesign with improved visuals and functionality.

items.title: Enhance i18n Support with New Factories in Common Frontend 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces new i18n factories, including `makeFlatLoader` and `makeNestedLoader`, to streamline localization processes. Additionally, the `createSetI18nLanguage` function has been added to manage locale settings effectively. The changes also include the addition of optional peer dependencies for Vue and vue-i18n, along with a new `/i18n` subpath and comprehensive testing, ensuring robust functionality across applications.

items.title: Modernize AuthFrontend Build and SSR Features in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions AuthFrontend to use vite-ssg with server-side rendering (SSR) capabilities, enhancing the login page experience. Key changes include upgrading to Vite 7 and Vue 3.5, implementing a runtime flag for SSR, and ensuring compatibility with existing dependencies. The build process has been streamlined, and the application now supports pre-rendered pages based on user preferences without requiring a rebuild.

items.title: SSR-safe foundation and login page enhancements in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update establishes an SSR-safe foundation for Nexior by integrating ViteSSG with a runtime `features=ssr` flag. Key changes include the pre-rendering of the login page, modifications to the router and store, and updates to the Dockerfile and nginx configuration for serving the appropriate content based on the flag. This sets the stage for future enhancements to interactive app pages.

items.title: Enhance Legal Pages with SSR Support in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update extends the SSR capabilities to the legal pages, including /terms, /privacy, and /support. Key modifications include fixes to the VueMarkdown component to prevent crashes during SSG builds and updates to the nginx configuration and vite settings to accommodate the new routes. The legal pages will now fetch their body client-side while the frame is rendered server-side, improving performance and user experience.

items.title: Streamline HTTP Client with Core Integration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update completes the core adoption of the HTTP interceptor in PlatformFrontend by replacing the custom axios instance with the `createHttpClient` from @acedatacloud/core. Key features include maintaining exact parity with previous behavior while enhancing the handling of authorization and error tracking. The changes are validated with CI checks and unit tests, ensuring a smooth transition.

items.title: Revamped UI with Real Logos and Package Selector in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a redesigned directory that aligns with the overall site aesthetic, utilizing native Element Plus components for a consistent user experience. Key enhancements include the integration of actual provider logos, a new recharge-amount selector for credit packages, and adherence to updated design tokens for improved visual appeal. All changes have been successfully linted and built without issues.

items.title: Enhance Catalog with Real Logos and Pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces real brand logos and raw credit costs for items in the catalog, allowing for dynamic pricing based on a full 8-tier credit to USD ladder. The changes ensure that the UI can display accurate pricing at any recharge amount, enhancing user experience. Additionally, tests have been updated to reflect these new features.

items.title: Enhance Slack Integration with Meta Tag in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `<meta>` tag for the Slack App ID in the site's header, enabling Slack to suggest our app when links are shared. The addition is part of the Slack Marketplace distribution setup and does not alter existing functionality. The App ID is public and enhances our app's visibility in Slack workspaces.

items.title: Refactor telemetry and fingerprint integration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the AuthFrontend by integrating telemetry and fingerprint functionalities from the @acedatacloud/core package. Key changes include the introduction of `createTelemetry` for telemetry tracking and `createFingerprintResolver` for fingerprint resolution, both maintaining existing function names. The implementation passes all build checks and linting, ensuring a smooth transition to the new core functionalities.

items.title: Refactor to Use Core Modules in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the `@acedatacloud/core` package into AuthFrontend, marking its first consumer. Key changes include the refactoring of chunk load error handling and locale retrieval functions, while maintaining existing functionality. The build process has been verified to pass without issues, ensuring a smooth transition to the new module structure.

items.title: Enhance Media Pricing Display in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a new pricing display for media cards, showcasing a min-max range instead of a single price point. The changes leverage the catalog's new min/max fields, improving clarity for users selecting tiers. Additionally, linting and build processes have been successfully validated.

items.title: Enhance Media Pricing Display in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the media pricing display by replacing the misleading 'from cheapest op' with a comprehensive min-max credit range for each media service. The catalog now accurately reflects the full pricing spectrum at both tiers, ensuring users have a clearer understanding of costs. Tests have been updated to validate these changes.

items.title: Launch SSG Runtime Features for Home and /x402 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update completes Phase 1 of the SSG plan by implementing a runtime flag for SSG, allowing for dynamic toggling without redeploying. Key changes include modifications to the Dockerfile, nginx configuration, and the addition of a new script for generating SPA shells. This approach enhances user experience by enabling gradual feature rollout and easy rollback options.

items.title: Launch Phase 1 of SSG for Home and /x402 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update completes Phase 1 of the static site generation (SSG) initiative, enabling server-rendered first paint for the home and /x402 pages. The feature is controlled by a build flag, ensuring that production remains unaffected until the flag is enabled. Key changes include modifications to the Dockerfile, docker-compose.yaml, and vite.config.ts to support this new functionality.

items.title: Introducing SSG Capabilities for Enhanced Performance in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the foundation for Static Site Generation (SSG) using vite-ssg, allowing for build-time rendering without affecting the current production setup. Key changes include the addition of a new build command for SSG, modifications to the router for SSR compatibility, and adjustments to ensure that the production environment remains unchanged. Future increments will expand SSG capabilities and prepare for a full production cutover.

items.title: Enhance A1 SSG Plan for Instant First Paint in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a detailed implementation plan for the A1 workstream, focusing on server-side generation (SSG) for public pages. Key changes include a feature-flag safety model, allowing for controlled rollouts, and a phased approach to implementation. The plan aims to provide users with an instant, content-rich first paint while maintaining interactivity, significantly improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance homepage with real SLA uptime card in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update reintroduces the homepage uptime stat card, now powered by actual data from the `api_usage.json` snapshot instead of a static value. The card will only display when real uptime data is available, ensuring users see accurate information. The current uptime value is approximately 99.7% over the last 6 hours.

items.title: Enhanced SLA Metrics for API Usage in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a real, request-weighted SLA for the API usage snapshot, adding `sla.last_6h` and `sla.last_24h` metrics. These metrics provide accurate uptime calculations by excluding synthetic probe data and only counting 5xx errors as downtime. The changes ensure that the homepage will eventually reflect these real-time SLA values, enhancing transparency and reliability.

items.title: Revamped Multi-Modal Directory and Pricing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a multi-modal directory featuring various modalities including chat, image, video, music, and search, expanding the model count from 55 to 78. It also implements package-aware tier pricing, allowing for dynamic pricing based on usage tiers. The redesign enhances the user interface with a professional card-grid gallery and improved accessibility features.

items.title: Enhance Multi-Modal Model Catalog with Tiered Pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive multi-modal model catalog, featuring 78 items across five modalities, each with entry and best tier pricing. The new API endpoint allows users to filter models by modality and ensures accurate pricing based on real package tiers. Additionally, tests have been added to verify the multi-modality and tiered pricing functionality.

items.title: Refactor to Integrate @acedatacloud/core in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the first implementation of the shared @acedatacloud/core in the PlatformFrontend, aligning with the Nexior rollout. Key changes include the renaming of utility functions and the introduction of behavior-preserving shims, resulting in a reduction of 200 lines of code. The integration enhances functionality while maintaining existing interfaces, ensuring a smooth transition for developers.

items.title: New Models and Pricing Directory Launch in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new, SEO-friendly directory for models and pricing, accessible via the `/models` and `/pricing` endpoints. This feature includes a searchable directory with real-time pricing comparisons and savings badges, enhancing user experience and transparency. The implementation is flag-gated, ensuring a safe rollout while supporting multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance chunk-load guard for entry signatures in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update broadens the chunk-load entry-signature regex to accommodate both `index-<hash>.js` and `index.<hash>.js` formats. This change ensures that the stale-chunk reload guard functions correctly for the PlatformFrontend's dotted entry. The update is backward-compatible with Nexior on versions `^0.2.0`, and includes 22 new tests that have all passed.

items.title: Launch Public Pricing API and Sitemap for Models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a public per-model pricing API, allowing users to access pricing details for each model via the new endpoint `/api/v1/models/?with_pricing=1`. The pricing is dynamically calculated based on a single source of truth, ensuring accuracy and consistency with billing. Additionally, a sitemap and robots.txt have been added to enhance discoverability for the `/models` and `/pricing` directories.

items.title: Fix prerender for HTTPS URLs to enhance bot rendering in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This hotfix addresses an issue where bots received an empty HTML response due to incorrect URL handling in the prerender setup. The rewrite rule has been updated to ensure that HTTPS URLs are always used, allowing bots to receive the correct rendered content. Verification shows that the changes successfully render the expected JSON-LD output without affecting human users.

items.title: Pricing adjustments for video services in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update implements significant price reductions for grok-video and midjourney-video services based on a recent profitability audit. Key changes include lowering prices to align more closely with competitor rates while maintaining healthy margins. The adjustments are expected to enhance competitiveness and attract more users to these services.

items.title: Deprecate Sora APIs as Service Goes Offline in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update marks the Sora APIs as deprecated following the service being taken offline due to consistent failures. The two API routes, `/sora/videos` and `/sora/tasks`, have been removed from the documentation and will now return a 404 error. The changes are reversible, allowing for potential restoration in the future.

items.title: Revamped Slack Marketplace Tracker in Index 🚀
items.summary: The Slack Marketplace plan documentation has been refreshed to serve as a live follow-up tracker, eliminating the outdated duplicate. Key updates include a redefined app status, trimmed OAuth scopes, and completion of all submission steps, although a blocker remains due to the requirement of at least five active workspaces for app review submission.

items.title: Enhance Crawlable Models & Pricing Directory in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a detailed plan for a crawlable `/models` and `/pricing` directory, enhancing discoverability as outlined in the INFLUENCE-AND-DISCOVERABILITY strategy. It leverages the existing public API and integrates per-model pricing, ensuring a single source of truth. The new features will be deployed behind a feature flag, allowing for controlled rollout and verification across multiple build PRs.

items.title: Transition to Self-Hosted Prerender for Enhanced Crawler Visibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully migrates the frontend to a self-hosted prerender solution, replacing the previous reliance on prerender.io. Key changes include the update of the PRERENDER_URL to point to the in-cluster service and the removal of the unused PRERENDER_TOKEN. This transition ensures that bots receive fully-rendered HTML, improving visibility and performance without incurring additional costs.

items.title: Final Core Adoption for HTTP Client in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update finalizes the integration of the core HTTP client for Nexior, replacing the previous axios instance with `createHttpClient` from `@acedatacloud/core`. Key features such as request headers and error handling remain consistent, ensuring seamless functionality. The change includes extensive testing coverage and has been validated with linting and build checks.

items.title: Clean up ACE extension link in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the redundant `?category=development` from the ACE extension guide link in the BYOC credentials dialog. The link now directly points to the document, leveraging tag-derivation for the sidebar category. This change is cosmetic, as the old links remain functional.

items.title: Enhance Slack Integration with New Meta Tag in Nexior 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a new meta tag for Slack App Suggestions by adding `<meta name="slack-app-id" content="A0B0VU3KBQX" />` to the site's `<head>`. This change will help surface our Slack app as a suggestion when links from this domain are shared in Slack workspaces. The addition does not alter existing behavior and the App ID is public.

items.title: Streamline chunk-load recovery with @acedatacloud/core in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the chunk-load error handling in Nexior by integrating the createChunkLoadErrorHandler from the @acedatacloud/core package. The existing functionality remains unchanged, ensuring seamless operation while reducing the codebase by 90 lines. This marks the first implementation of the shared core package within Nexior, with successful builds confirmed using vue-tsc and vite.

items.title: Streamline Telemetry and Fingerprint Integration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the telemetry and fingerprint functionalities in Nexior by integrating two core modules from @acedatacloud/core. The telemetry now utilizes `createTelemetry`, significantly reducing code size, while the fingerprinting process leverages `createFingerprintResolver` with lazy loading to optimize performance. Additionally, the dependency on @acedatacloud/core has been updated to version 0.2.0.

items.title: Streamline Document URLs and Sidebar Categories in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the documentation page by deriving sidebar categories directly from document tags, eliminating the need for `?category=` in URLs. A new utility has been introduced to manage category mapping, ensuring clean and shareable links while maintaining existing functionality for legacy URLs. The implementation also improves user experience by preventing sidebar flashes on cold loads.

items.title: Enhance Type Checking with typesVersions in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a `typesVersions` map for various subpaths in the core package, addressing type-checking issues for apps using `moduleResolution: "node"`. The version has been bumped from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0, ensuring compatibility with TypeScript 5.8. All changes have been verified, and the runtime remains stable with Vite.

items.title: Pricing & Margin Audit Insights in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive pricing and margin audit for 34 services and 254 SKUs, revealing that while we are cheaper than competitors on approximately 120 SKUs, nearly 48% of our offerings are priced below upstream costs. Key issues identified include credit-value de-anchoring and placeholder pricing strategies. The report outlines a remediation roadmap aimed at addressing these challenges and optimizing our pricing strategy.

items.title: Introducing a Self-Hosted Prerender Service in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a self-hosted headless-Chromium prerender service to replace the paid prerender.io, which was returning 503 errors. The new service is additive and does not alter the existing PRERENDER_URL, ensuring current functionality remains intact. Future updates will switch the URL to the new service after successful deployment verification.

items.title: Updated Plan Statuses and Archived Shipped Plans in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the statuses of active plans by verifying them against merged PRs and live data, resulting in the archiving of 8 plans that have already been shipped. The README has been rewritten to reflect these changes and now includes new plans from parallel work. Additionally, a critical security issue has been identified that requires immediate attention.

items.title: Package renamed to @acedatacloud/core in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the package from `@acedatacloud/common-frontend` to `@acedatacloud/core`, streamlining the scope name. Key files such as package.json, README, and the release workflow have been updated accordingly. There are no behavioral changes, and all tests are passing successfully.

items.title: Enhance Homepage Stats and Prerender Timeout in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes hardcoded fabricated statistics from the homepage, ensuring that metrics are only displayed when backed by real data. Additionally, the prerender timeout for bot user agents has been increased from 10 seconds to 30 seconds, improving the crawlability of heavy pages. These changes aim to enhance trust and discoverability for users.

items.title: Enhance Influence & Discoverability Strategy in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive analysis and phased execution plan aimed at addressing the influence and discoverability challenges faced by AceDataCloud. Key changes include the addition of a new document detailing the current gaps and a structured plan to enhance crawlability, content visibility, and community trust. Immediate actions focus on fixing broken elements and eliminating misleading statistics to improve overall platform reach.

items.title: Transform CommonFrontend into NPM Package in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update pivots CommonFrontend to a published NPM package, `@acedatacloud/common-frontend`, consolidating shared infrastructure across multiple applications. Key features include a config-driven architecture for HTTP clients, telemetry, fingerprinting, and locale management, all built with TypeScript and designed for optimal tree-shaking. The CI/CD workflows are set up for automated testing and publishing, enhancing the development process.

items.title: Streamlined Planning Docs in Index 📚
items.summary: This update consolidates all planning documents into a single, organized `plans/` directory, enhancing navigability and clarity. Key changes include the deletion of 5 outdated documents, archiving of 8 reference docs, and the grouping of approximately 20 active plans into themed folders. Legal artifacts have also been moved to a dedicated `_legal/` folder, ensuring better compliance tracking.

items.title: Unifying Claude.ai MCP Guides for Flux and Sora in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the documentation for the Flux and Sora MCP guides under the correct naming convention, ensuring they are properly nested within the Claude.ai/Desktop MCP series. Key changes include renaming files, updating cross-links across 93 files, and correcting titles to match the series. This enhances the organization and accessibility of the documentation for users.

items.title: Enhance Slack Integration with New Meta Tag in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `<meta>` tag for the Slack app ID in the site's `<head>`, enabling Slack to suggest our app when links are shared. The addition of this tag does not alter existing behaviors but enhances visibility in the Slack Marketplace. The app ID is public and serves as a key component of our distribution setup.

items.title: Streamline Skill Management by Moving Marketing Video Factory in Skills 🎥
items.summary: The marketing-video-factory skill has been successfully removed from the customer Skills package and relocated to PlatformStudio. This change aligns the orchestration playbook with its implementation, reducing drift and enhancing clarity. The single-service skills it orchestrates remain unchanged in the customer package.

items.title: Streamline Marketing Video Production in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update moves the marketing-video-factory orchestration skill into PlatformStudio, ensuring it is closely aligned with its implementation. Key additions include a comprehensive playbook and shared documentation for authentication and async tasks, enhancing usability and self-containment. The single-service skills remain unchanged in the customer Skills package, maintaining a clear separation of concerns.

items.title: Streamlined Slack Scopes and Connector Insights in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update documents the successful trimming of Slack app scopes, reducing bot scopes from 72 to 15 and user scopes to zero. Key findings reveal that the app functions as a data connector rather than a traditional in-Slack bot, necessitating a reinstall for the new scopes to take effect. Additional details about privacy and terms have also been included in the documentation.

items.title: Enhance TTS Pricing Display in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the rendering of TTS pricing on the service pricing page by replacing the raw JSON display with a user-friendly format. The changes include unwrapping the minimum charge guard and displaying rates per 1M bytes instead of per byte. Additionally, it addresses similar issues for the Fish TTS API, ensuring consistency across the platform.

items.title: Retire openai-audio service and merge TTS pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The standalone openai-audio service has been retired and its API has been successfully integrated into the openai service. Key changes include the deletion of the openai-audio service and the addition of TTS pricing rules to the openai service's display pricing. This streamlines our services and ensures that TTS pricing is now clearly listed under the openai service.

items.title: Revamp Talking Photo i18n and Model Requirements in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update fixes several issues with the Talking Photo feature, including translation errors, model requirements, and layout inconsistencies. Key changes include the proper wrapping of i18n keys, ensuring the model is always sent during generation, and adding model and mode selectors for improved user experience. These enhancements ensure a smoother and more reliable functionality for users.
